critters.name: White-tailed Deer
critters.summary: Early-summer leaf-line regulars browsing the green buffet while fawns stay tucked like tiny woodland secrets.
critters.fact: By late June, does often keep fawns hidden in tall grass or cover while adults feed heavily on lush leaves, forbs, and tender shoots.
critters.activeFact: Right now, deer are working shaded edges, fresh browse, and quiet openings, with fawns likely nearby even when you never see them.

critters.name: Black Bear
critters.summary: Big summer forager roaming the groceries harder now that berries, bugs, and green growth are all joining the menu.
critters.fact: Black bears feed heavily in early summer on grasses, insects, larvae, carrion, and emerging soft mast as they rebuild body reserves.
critters.activeFact: Right now, bear activity is most likely near berry patches, rotting logs, swamp edges, and quiet feeding lanes with easy calories.

critters.name: Coyote
critters.summary: Lean summer patrol unit working edges while young-of-year prey turns the woods into a louder, softer buffet.
critters.fact: Coyotes feed opportunistically in early summer, hunting rodents, rabbits, birds, insects, and vulnerable young animals while pups grow at dens.
critters.activeFact: Right now, coyotes may be traveling low-light routes near fields, two-tracks, and brushy edges where prey movement stays steady.

critters.name: Red Fox

critters.fact: Red fox adults often hunt heavily in early summer to feed growing kits, focusing on rodents, rabbits, insects, and birds.
critters.activeFact: Right now, foxes may be seen slipping through openings or carrying prey back toward denning cover like tiny red delivery drivers.

critters.name: Gray Fox

critters.fact: Gray foxes use dense leafed-out cover in early summer to travel, hunt, and protect young while staying mostly hidden.
critters.activeFact: Right now, gray fox movement is most likely inside thickets, wooded edges, and tangled cover where cameras only get lucky glimpses.

critters.name: Bobcat
critters.summary: Silent summer stalker moving through thick cover like the woods signed a nondisclosure agreement.
critters.fact: Bobcats hunt rabbits, squirrels, birds, and small mammals through early summer, using heavy vegetation and edge habitat for ambush.
critters.activeFact: Right now, bobcats may be working shaded transitions and brushy travel lanes where young animals and active prey are easier to find.

critters.name: Porcupine
critters.summary: Slow green-season browser finally enjoying softer leaves without giving up the whole cranky tree-goblin lifestyle.
critters.fact: Porcupines feed more on leaves, herbs, and soft vegetation in summer while still using trees for shelter and woody foods.
critters.activeFact: Right now, porcupines may be found browsing leafy branches or loafing in trees where nobody is rushing their ancient little agenda.

critters.name: Beaver
critters.summary: Dam-site project manager deep in summer maintenance while water levels and fresh growth keep the crew employed.
critters.fact: Beavers continue cutting fresh vegetation and repairing dams through summer, especially where changing water levels threaten pond control.
critters.activeFact: Right now, fresh peeled sticks, muddy slides, and overnight repairs are signs the beaver department is still very much open.

critters.name: Muskrat
critters.summary: Marsh commuter cruising pond lanes now that aquatic salad season has officially clocked in.
critters.fact: Muskrats feed heavily on aquatic plants in summer and may raise young in bank burrows or vegetation lodges.
critters.activeFact: Right now, muskrats may be swimming shoreline routes, clipping greens, or popping through cattails like little wetland interns.

critters.name: Mink
critters.summary: Shoreline troublemaker hunting the wet edges where frogs, fish, and bad decisions gather.
critters.fact: Minks patrol summer banks for frogs, fish, crayfish, voles, eggs, and young birds, using dense shoreline cover to stay hidden.
critters.activeFact: Right now, mink activity is most likely along muddy pond edges, creek banks, and narrow trails where everything smells like lunch.

critters.name: Fisher
critters.summary: Deep-woods shadow threading green timber like it knows where the quiet keeps its valuables.
critters.fact: Fishers remain active year-round and use early-summer forest cover to hunt squirrels, hares, birds, and small mammals.
critters.activeFact: Right now, fishers may be moving through heavier timber and shaded corridors, usually noticed only by tracks or trail-cam luck.

critters.name: River Otter
critters.summary: Creek-and-pond acrobat turning summer water into a snack lane with a splashy attitude problem.
critters.fact: River otters hunt fish, frogs, crayfish, and aquatic prey through summer and often travel between ponds, streams, and wetlands.
critters.activeFact: Right now, otters may be cruising open water, sliding banks, or working fish-heavy corners with full pond-gremlin confidence.

critters.name: Eastern Gray Squirrel

critters.fact: Eastern gray squirrels feed on buds, seeds, fungi, insects, and early fruits in summer while young squirrels become more visible.
critters.activeFact: Right now, gray squirrels are racing through full canopy, raiding feeders, and teaching young ones the fine art of unnecessary drama.

critters.name: American Red Squirrel
critters.summary: Tiny red siren still yelling at the forest like he personally invented property rights.
critters.fact: American red squirrels defend middens, nests, and feeding areas year-round, with summer activity focused on seeds, fungi, and young growth.
critters.activeFact: Right now, red squirrels are loud in conifers and mixed woods, scolding everything with a pulse and several things without one.

critters.name: Chipmunk
critters.summary: Pocket rocket of the trail edge stuffing cheeks like summer is a grocery challenge.
critters.fact: Chipmunks spend early summer feeding on seeds, berries, fungi, insects, and plant material while keeping burrow systems active.
critters.activeFact: Right now, chipmunks are darting around logs, stumps, and trail edges with cheeks full and zero respect for traffic patterns.

critters.name: Snowshoe Hare

critters.activeFact: Right now, hares are most likely tucked in thick cover, where green growth finally gives them the camouflage they ordered months ago.

critters.name: Eastern Cottontail
critters.summary: Brush-pile hopper living close to the salad bar and even closer to the escape hatch.
critters.fact: Eastern cottontails feed on grasses, clover, weeds, and garden-edge greens in summer while relying on thick cover for protection.
critters.activeFact: Right now, cottontails are active along mowed edges, brush piles, and fresh growth pockets, especially early and late in the day.

critters.name: Raccoon
critters.summary: Night-shift camp inspector checking ponds, feeders, logs, and anything foolish enough to smell edible.
critters.fact: Raccoons forage widely in summer for insects, frogs, eggs, fruit, crayfish, fish, and human leftovers when available.
critters.activeFact: Right now, raccoons may be working pond edges, creek crossings, feeders, and camp margins like furry little auditors with hands.

critters.name: Opossum
critters.summary: Warm-night wanderer finally operating in weather that doesn’t feel like a personal insult.
critters.fact: Opossums feed on insects, carrion, fruit, small animals, and ticks through summer while traveling after dark through edge habitat.
critters.activeFact: Right now, opossums may show around brush, sheds, trails, and water edges, quietly cleaning up the weird buffet nobody else wants.

critters.name: Striped Skunk
critters.summary: Striped lawn inspector rooting up soft ground with one built-in warning label and no fear of management.
critters.fact: Striped skunks feed heavily on insects, grubs, worms, eggs, and small animals during summer nights.
critters.activeFact: Right now, skunks may be digging in lawns, trail edges, and damp soil where the grub counter is open late.

critters.name: Flying Squirrel
critters.summary: After-dark canopy glider running secret squirrel business above the whole daylight circus.
critters.fact: Flying squirrels feed on nuts, seeds, fungi, buds, insects, and bird eggs in summer while nesting in cavities or boxes.

critters.name: Little Brown Bat
critters.summary: Small dusk bug vacuum finally getting decent insect traffic over ponds and open lanes.
critters.fact: Little brown bats feed heavily on flying insects during warm summer evenings, often foraging over water and woodland openings.
critters.activeFact: Right now, mild nights may bring quick bat loops over ponds, trails, and edges where mosquitoes made poor career choices.

critters.name: Big Brown Bat
critters.summary: Tough summer moth hunter patrolling lights, tree lines, and clearings like pest control with wings.
critters.fact: Big brown bats feed on beetles, moths, flies, and other night insects, often using buildings or trees as roost sites.

critters.name: Barred Owl

critters.name: Great Horned Owl

critters.name: Blue Jay
critters.summary: Blue feathered alarm system policing the canopy like every nest, feeder, and shadow requires a report.
critters.fact: Blue jays are active nest defenders in summer and may mob predators while feeding on insects, seeds, eggs, and fruit.
critters.activeFact: Right now, jays are loud in the leafed-out woods, chasing rivals and announcing anything suspicious before anyone asked.

critters.name: Northern Cardinal
critters.summary: Red morning announcer still belting from brushy cover like sunrise comes with a contract.
critters.fact: Northern cardinals may raise multiple broods in a season, with males singing and helping feed mates and young.

critters.name: Black-capped Chickadee
critters.summary: Tiny cavity-nest manager running bug deliveries like the nestlings filed a hunger complaint.
critters.fact: Black-capped chickadees feed caterpillars and insects to nestlings in spring and early summer after nesting in cavities.
critters.activeFact: Right now, chickadees may be making quick trips through branches with insects, keeping the tiny-bird household operational.

critters.name: Tufted Titmouse
critters.summary: Gray little branch commentator moving through summer leaves with opinions and nest errands.
critters.fact: Tufted titmice nest in cavities and feed insects, seeds, and berries, often staying active in family groups after young fledge.
critters.activeFact: Right now, titmice may be carrying food, calling from cover, and working leafy edges where bugs are plentiful.

critters.name: White-breasted Nuthatch
critters.summary: Upside-down trunk mechanic checking bark seams like the tree came with hidden snacks.
critters.fact: White-breasted nuthatches forage bark for insects and may continue feeding young around cavities and mature trees in early summer.
critters.activeFact: Right now, nuthatches are busy on big trunks, slipping headfirst down bark and grabbing bugs from places nobody else checks.

critters.name: Downy Woodpecker
critters.summary: Small bark drummer now deep into bug duty while summer hides the work behind leaves.
critters.fact: Downy woodpeckers excavate cavities and feed heavily on insects, often bringing larvae and ants to nestlings.

critters.name: Hairy Woodpecker
critters.summary: Bigger woodland hammer staying busy where old trees still hide the good insect stash.
critters.fact: Hairy woodpeckers hunt wood-boring insects and larvae in mature trees and may feed nestlings in early summer.
critters.activeFact: Right now, hairy woodpeckers are likely working taller timber, where sound gives them away before the leaves do.

critters.name: Pileated Woodpecker
critters.summary: Giant forest carpenter carving summer snacks out of deadwood with zero apology.
critters.fact: Pileated woodpeckers excavate large holes for carpenter ants and beetle larvae and rely on mature trees and deadwood.

critters.summary: Vocal bark brawler keeping the summer woods informed that his territory still has management.
critters.fact: Red-bellied woodpeckers feed on insects, nuts, fruit, and seeds while nesting in tree cavities during the warm season.
critters.activeFact: Right now, they may be calling, chasing, and working trunks around nest areas like a neighborhood argument with wings.

critters.name: Mourning Dove
critters.summary: Soft-voiced twig optimist proving summer nesting does not require structural standards.
critters.fact: Mourning doves can nest multiple times in warm months and feed their young crop milk before switching them to seeds.
critters.activeFact: Right now, paired doves may be cooing, feeding, and attempting another flimsy nest with absolute confidence.

critters.name: American Goldfinch
critters.summary: Bright little seed specialist finally dressed closer to summer and waiting on the best weed crop.

critters.activeFact: Right now, goldfinches are brighter and livelier, feeding on seeds and scouting the weedy patches they’ll care about even more soon.

critters.name: Dark-eyed Junco
critters.summary: Cool-season ground picker mostly fading from the local spotlight as summer steals the stage.

critters.activeFact: Right now, juncos are likely much less common around feeders, with summer birds and young families taking over the ground scene.

critters.name: House Finch
critters.summary: Backyard singer juggling nest life, feeder stops, and red-feathered confidence near the edges.
critters.fact: House finches may nest multiple times in a season and feed seeds, buds, and plant matter to their young.
critters.activeFact: Right now, house finches are active around feeders, buildings, shrubs, and edges, with plenty of singing and family movement.

critters.name: Purple Finch
critters.summary: Rosy woodland singer drifting through leafy cover like the forest kept him on limited release.
critters.fact: Purple finches breed in northern forests and feed on seeds, buds, berries, and insects during the warm season.
critters.activeFact: Right now, purple finches may be quieter and more scattered, showing up in wooded areas where food and cover line up.

critters.name: Wild Turkey
critters.summary: Big ground bird shifting from strut theater into nesting, brooding, and tall-grass sneaking.
critters.fact: By early summer, many turkey hens are nesting or caring for poults while gobbler display activity fades from peak spring levels.

critters.name: Ruffed Grouse
critters.summary: Forest drummer turning down the spring concert while cover fills in and broods become the real business.
critters.fact: Ruffed grouse chicks hatch in late spring or early summer and feed heavily on insects during their first weeks.
critters.activeFact: Right now, drumming may be tapering, while hens and young stay deep in thick cover where insects are plentiful.

critters.name: Sandhill Crane
critters.summary: Tall marshland megaphone now deep into nesting territory and family-season seriousness.
critters.fact: Sandhill cranes nest in wetlands and raise precocial chicks that can walk soon after hatching.
critters.activeFact: Right now, crane pairs may be guarding wetland territories or moving with young nearby, loud enough to announce the whole operation.

critters.name: American Crow
critters.summary: Black-feathered field auditor running summer surveillance with a family crew and questionable curiosity.
critters.fact: American crows feed young in spring and early summer and use family groups to mob predators and search for food.
critters.activeFact: Right now, crows are investigating fields, roads, nests, and anything suspicious with the confidence of unpaid inspectors.

critters.name: Common Raven
critters.summary: Big timber philosopher cruising summer air like it already knows how the story ends.

critters.name: Red-tailed Hawk
critters.summary: Airborne landlord watching summer fields while nest duty turns into hungry-young logistics.
critters.fact: Red-tailed hawk young grow through late spring and early summer, while adults hunt steadily to feed them.
critters.activeFact: Right now, red-tails may be circling over openings or perching near nest zones, scanning for rodents, snakes, and careless movement.

critters.name: Cooper’s Hawk
critters.summary: Woodland speed demon threading leafy lanes where songbirds suddenly remember urgency.
critters.fact: Cooper’s hawks nest in wooded areas and hunt birds through dense cover with fast, agile flight.

critters.name: Mallard Duck
critters.summary: Classic pond duck shifting from pair-season drama into nesting cover and duckling-watch mode.
critters.fact: Mallards nest on the ground near water, and ducklings feed themselves soon after hatching while following the hen.

critters.name: Wood Duck
critters.summary: Flashy backwater specialist turning tree-cavity success into tiny duckling chaos on the water.
critters.fact: Wood duck ducklings leap from tree cavities shortly after hatching and follow the hen to water.

critters.name: Canada Goose
critters.summary: Shoreline authority figure now escorting fuzzy goslings with maximum attitude and no customer service training.
critters.fact: Canada geese hatch goslings in spring, and both parents defend the young aggressively while they feed on grasses and aquatic plants.
critters.activeFact: Right now, geese with goslings are loud, defensive, and fully prepared to make the pond feel like their private property.

critters.name: Great Blue Heron

critters.activeFact: Right now, herons may be posted along pond edges, creeks, and marshes, waiting for one unlucky twitch below the surface.

critters.name: Painted Turtle

critters.name: Bluegill
critters.summary: Pond panfish now sliding into bed-making season with tiny territorial attitudes.
critters.fact: Bluegills spawn in warm shallows, where males build and guard circular nests in colonies.
critters.activeFact: Right now, bluegill may be gathering near shallow beds, guarding spots, and acting tougher than their size suggests.

critters.name: Largemouth Bass
critters.summary: Shallow-water bruiser guarding beds and ambush lanes with big-mouth confidence.
critters.fact: Largemouth bass spawn in warm shallows, and males often guard nests and newly hatched fry.

critters.name: Yellow Perch
critters.summary: Striped school fish settling into summer patterns after the early-season rush.
critters.fact: Yellow perch spawn earlier in spring and shift toward feeding groups around vegetation, drop-offs, and cooler structure as summer builds.
critters.activeFact: Right now, perch may be cruising weeds and nearshore structure, feeding in groups where small prey gathers.

critters.name: Northern Pike

critters.fact: Northern pike spawn early and then use vegetation, cooler water, and ambush cover through summer feeding periods.

critters.name: Spring Peeper

critters.fact: Spring peepers call most intensely in spring, with activity tapering as breeding winds down and summer frogs take more of the stage.

critters.name: American Bullfrog

critters.name: Green Frog

critters.fact: Green frogs become more vocal in late spring and summer, calling from pond edges, marshes, and slow water.

critters.name: Leopard Frog

critters.fact: Leopard frogs use wet meadows, marsh edges, and grassy cover in early summer while feeding on insects and small invertebrates.
critters.activeFact: Right now, leopard frogs may be active near ditches, pond edges, and wet grass, launching themselves the second feet get too close.

critters.name: Garter Snake
critters.summary: Striped sun noodle now fully active and patrolling edges for frogs, worms, and poor decisions.

critters.name: Water Snake

critters.fact: Water snakes become more visible in warm weather, hunting fish and amphibians near ponds, streams, and marshes.
critters.activeFact: Right now, water snakes may be basking on banks, logs, and rocks before dropping into the water like they own the dock.

critters.summary: Secretive cover snake working warm edges while mice make questionable summer choices.
critters.fact: Eastern milk snakes hunt rodents, small snakes, and other prey, often using barns, boards, rocks, and brushy cover.
critters.activeFact: Right now, milk snakes may turn up under warm cover or along brushy edges where rodents are active.

critters.name: Monarch Butterfly
critters.summary: Orange summer traveler finally becoming more realistic as milkweed and warmer days enter the chat.

critters.activeFact: Right now, monarch sightings are more possible around milkweed, open fields, and sunny wildflower edges.

critters.name: Swallowtail Butterfly
critters.summary: Big-winged flower cruiser finally getting the warm afternoons it was waiting for.

critters.name: Dragonfly

critters.fact: Dragonflies emerge from aquatic nymphs as temperatures rise and become major predators of mosquitoes, flies, and other insects.
critters.activeFact: Right now, dragonflies may be patrolling pond edges, trails, and openings where flying insects are making themselves available.

critters.summary: Delicate wetland patrol now hovering low around reeds like the pond hired tiny security.
critters.fact: Damselflies emerge near water in late spring and summer and hunt small flying insects close to vegetation.

critters.name: Firefly
critters.summary: Summer light crew starting to warm up backstage for those first blink-and-grin nights.
critters.fact: Fireflies begin flashing when warm, humid summer evenings arrive, using light patterns to find mates.
critters.activeFact: Right now, early firefly activity may be starting or just around the corner in damp grass, field edges, and woodland openings.

critters.name: Honeybee
critters.summary: Flower-route worker fully clocked in now that blooms are finally worth the paperwork.
critters.fact: Honeybees forage heavily in warm weather, gathering nectar and pollen from flowering plants and communicating food locations by dance.
critters.activeFact: Right now, honeybees may be active around clover, garden blooms, fruit blossoms, and sunny wildflower patches.

critters.name: Bumblebee
critters.summary: Fuzzy flower tank now moving from queen scouting into full colony-building season.
critters.fact: Bumblebee queens establish nests in spring, and worker numbers grow as summer flowers provide more nectar and pollen.
critters.activeFact: Right now, bumblebees may be working clover, blossoms, and field edges, with the first colony workforce joining the queens.

critters.name: Grasshopper
critters.summary: Summer leg-spring rookie finally stepping onto the stage as grass gets taller and warmer.
critters.fact: Grasshoppers become more active as warm-season vegetation develops, feeding on grasses and leaves while growing through nymph stages.
